adjudications.basis: Reread ts-contacts-service/src/main/java/contacts/config/SecurityConfig.java:47-56 — corsConfigurer() bean registers /** with allowedOrigins/Methods/Headers=ALL, allowCredentials(false), maxAge(3600). Pass-b's line-47 citation is the same bean; kept as one atom with union refs.

Reread SecurityConfig.java:72-73 — antMatchers list swagger-ui.html, webjars/**, images/**, configuration/**, swagger-resources/**, v2/** as permitAll. Pass-b's object dropped swagger-resources; kept pass-a's fuller enumeration as canonical, same evidence.
Reread SecurityConfig.java:71 — antMatchers("/api/v1/contactservice/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN","USER"). Same line cited by both passes, only phrasing differs.

Reread ContactsController.java:34,41,49,58,65,72,80 — @CrossOrigin(origins="*") repeated on every mutating/lookup endpoint (all but /contacts/welcome). Pass-b cited only line 34; union refs kept, same fact.
Reread ts-execute-service/src/main/java/execute/config/SecurityConfig.java:47-56 — identical corsConfigurer() bean shape as contacts service. Same fact as pass-b's line-47 citation.
Reread execute/config/SecurityConfig.java:72-73 — same permitAll matcher list as contacts service. Pass-b omitted swagger-resources from the object text; kept fuller enumeration.
Reread execute/config/SecurityConfig.java:71 — antMatchers("/api/v1/executeservice/**").hasAnyRole("ADMIN","USER"). Same line, phrasing-only drift.

Reread ExecuteControlller.java:30,38 — @CrossOrigin(origins="*") on executeTicket and collectTicket. No matching pass-b atom for this controller's CORS (pass-b's onlyInPassB set has no execute.controller allows-cross-origin row); genuine unique finding.
Reread ContactsController.java:59 — @DeleteMapping(path="/contacts/{contactsId}"). Same endpoint as pass-b row, casing/hyphen drift only.
Reread ContactsController.java:35 — @GetMapping(path="/contacts"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:73 — @GetMapping(path="/contacts/account/{accountId}"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:29 — @GetMapping(path="/contacts/welcome"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:81 — @GetMapping(path="/contacts/{id}"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:42 — @PostMapping(path="/contacts"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:50 — @PostMapping(path="/contacts/admin"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ContactsController.java:66 — @PutMapping(path="/contacts"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.

Reread RabbitReceive.java:35 (@RabbitListener(queues=Queues.queueName)) and Queues.java:10 (queue name food_delivery). Consumption triggers a persistence side-effect (deliveryRepository.save at line 50), so handlesCommand is the accurate predicate; pass-b's servesQuery mislabels this fire-and-forget event consumer as a query responder. Canonicalized under pass-a's predicate.
Reread ExecuteControlller.java:39 — @GetMapping(path="/execute/collected/{orderId}"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ExecuteControlller.java:31 — @GetMapping(path="/execute/execute/{orderId}"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.
Reread ExecuteControlller.java:25 — @GetMapping(path="/welcome"). Same endpoint as pass-b row.

Reread Contacts.java:21 (@Table unique index on account_id/document_number/document_type), ContactsRepository.java:49 (native lookup by same triplet), ContactsServiceImpl.java:50,63 (createContacts/create both query the triplet and reject with status 0 before insert). No pass-b contract-axis atom exists for this chunk at all; genuine unique specification-level finding combining DB constraint and app-level enforcement.

Reread ContactsRepository.java:49 — @Query(value="SELECT * FROM contacts WHERE account_id=?1 AND document_number=?2 AND document_type=?3", nativeQuery=true). No pass-b contract-axis atom in this chunk; unique finding about the query's implementation shape.

Reread RabbitReceive.java:37 (JsonUtils.json2Object deserialize) and :39-42 (null check returns early before deliveryRepository.save at line 50). No pass-b contract-axis atom in this chunk; unique finding.

Reread DeliveryRepository.java:19 (findByOrderId(UUID orderId)) against Delivery.java:26-31, where id (String, @Id) and orderId (UUID) are distinct fields. No pass-b contract-axis atom in this chunk; unique finding.

Reread ContactsController.java:25 — @Autowired private ContactsService contactsService. Plain static field dependency, no restriction/cech/runtime evidence, so axis normalized to relation (pass-b had mislabeled it restriction).
Reread InitData.java:18 (@Autowired ContactsService service) and :29,:38 (service.create call sites). Pass-b cited only line 18; union refs kept, axis normalized to relation.
Reread ContactsServiceImpl.java:23 — @Autowired private ContactsRepository contactsRepository. Axis normalized to relation (plain field dependency, no restriction evidence).
Reread ContactsServiceImpl.java:20 — public class ContactsServiceImpl implements ContactsService. Axis normalized to relation.
Reread RabbitReceive.java:3 (import delivery.config.Queues) and :35 (@RabbitListener(queues=Queues.queueName)). Pass-b cited only line 35; union refs kept, axis normalized to relation.

Reread RabbitReceive.java:32 (@Autowired private DeliveryRepository deliveryRepository) and :50 (deliveryRepository.save call). Pass-b cited only line 32; union refs kept, axis normalized to relation.

Reread ExecuteControlller.java:3 (import execute.serivce.ExecuteService — source-verbatim package typo "serivce") and :21 (@Autowired private ExecuteService executeService). Pass-b cited only line 3; union refs kept, axis normalized to relation.

Reread ContactsServiceImpl.java findContactsById(33,37), createContacts(51,56), create(65,69), delete(79,83), modify(94,104), getAllContacts(111,115) — six distinct methods each with their own condition and message pair (e.g. "Already Exists" vs "Contacts already exists" vs "Contacts not found"). This single combined atom collapses six genuinely distinct branch semantics into one generic bucket, losing per-method specificity that the reread confirms are separate observations (alias preservation). Superseded by six finer adopted atoms.

Reread ContactsServiceImpl.java:55,69,102 (contactsRepository.save call sites), application.yml:11 (datasource block) and Contacts.java:21 (@Table unique index). Pass-b's paired row cited only the table/index angle (Contacts.java:21); combined into one persistsIn fact covering both the CRUD flow and the table shape.

Reread RabbitReceive.java:50 (deliveryRepository.save), DeliveryRepository.java:14 (CrudRepository interface) and application.yml:11 (datasource block), plus Delivery.java:29-34 (id/orderId/foodName/storeName/stationName fields) cited by pass-b's paired row. Combined into one persistsIn fact covering both the MQ-triggered CRUD flow and the entity's persisted field shape.

Reread Contacts.java:44-61 equals() — includes name, accountId, documentNumber, phoneNumber AND documentType, contradicting the claim 'not by name or phone' if read as the entity's actual Java identity. The cited refs (Contacts.java:21, ContactsRepository.java:49) only show the DB unique-index/native-query shape, already captured by the adopted contract atom atom:contract:contacts.entity.Contacts:unique-account-document-index; no service-layer use-site (ServiceImpl:50,63) is cited to ground an in-use identity claim distinct from that contract fact.
